flights.note: British Airways · LHR T5 → JFK T8
British Airways · JFK T8 → LHR T5

stops.name: Washington Square Park
stops.note: Street performers, iconic arch. NYU campus surrounds

stops.name: Rubirosa
stops.note: Thin crust vodka pie is the move. Cash only bar

stops.name: Ear Inn
stops.note: One of NYC's oldest bars (1817). No frills, great pints. 2 min from hotel

stops.name: The Django
stops.note: Live jazz in the Roxy Hotel basement. No cover, great cocktails. Locals' favourite

stops.name: Birch Coffee
stops.note: Local NYC chain, solid flat white

stops.name: Little Island
stops.address: Pier 55 Hudson River Park, New York
stops.note: Free timed tickets may be needed, check website
stops.alertNote: Check if free timed tickets are required

stops.name: High Line Walk
stops.note: Enter at Gansevoort St, walk north. Fully exposed — if windy/wet, cab to Chelsea Market instead

stops.name: Chelsea Market
stops.note: Los Tacos No.1 is the must-try. Gets busy at lunch

stops.name: MoMA
stops.note: FREE entry after 4pm (it's Friday!). Head back to hotel after Chelsea Market to rest, then hit MoMA at 4. Starry Night floor 5

stops.name: Bar Centrale
stops.note: Hidden above Joe Allen's, Broadway actors hang here

stops.name: Buena Vista Social Club
stops.note: Musical. Arrive 15 min early

stops.name: Round K
stops.note: Hong Kong–style coffee and egg waffles. Right by Chinatown

stops.name: Columbus Park Chinatown
stops.note: Morning tai chi, mahjong games to watch

stops.name: Doyers Street
stops.note: The 'Bloody Angle' — great photo op, old school barbers

stops.name: Nom Wah Tea Parlor
stops.note: NYC's oldest dim sum. Get the OG egg roll

stops.name: New Museum
stops.note: Contemporary art, striking building. Steps from Chinatown on Bowery. $18 entry, skip if tired

stops.name: Katz's Delicatessen
stops.note: Pastrami on rye since 1888. Hit it ~2-3pm when the lunch rush clears. 10 min walk from hotel. Cash only counter, card at table

stops.name: East Village Wander
stops.note: St Marks Place, Tompkins Square Park, quirky shops. Abraço for espresso (81 E 7th St). Chill before dinner

stops.name: Laser Wolf
stops.note: Uber from hotel ~15 min. Israeli grill, rooftop. The salatim spread is unreal

stops.name: Ivy Room Jazz Brunch
stops.note: Live jazz, cocktails. Smart casual

stops.name: Central Park South Walk
stops.note: Enter at Columbus Circle, walk along The Mall

stops.name: Death Becomes Her
stops.note: Musical. Lunt-Fontanne Theatre

stops.name: Joe's Pizza
stops.note: Classic NYC slice. Cash only, always a queue

stops.name: West Village Walk
stops.note: Perry St, Grove St for pretty brownstones. Friends building on Bedford

stops.name: Smalls Jazz Club
stops.note: Legendary basement jazz. $20 cover, BYOB. Sets from 7:30pm, late night jams after midnight

stops.name: Maman
stops.note: French-inspired café, locals love it. Great pastries. On the path to Brooklyn Bridge

stops.name: Brooklyn Bridge Park
stops.note: Walk the bridge from Manhattan side for best views

stops.name: Jane's Carousel
stops.note: $2 ride, glass pavilion by Jean Nouvel

stops.name: DUMBO Waterfront
stops.note: Washington St for the classic bridge-between-buildings shot

stops.name: Time Out Market
stops.note: Food hall, rooftop has great views. Clinton St Baking Co inside

stops.name: Vinegar Hill House
stops.note: Hidden gem on cobblestone streets near DUMBO. Farm-to-table, candlelit. Locals only know this one

stops.name: Harriet's Rooftop
stops.note: At 1 Hotel. Sunset cocktails with Manhattan skyline

stops.name: Butler Coffee
stops.note: Excellent cold brew, small but cosy

stops.name: Brooklyn Heights Promenade
stops.note: Best unobstructed Manhattan skyline view

stops.name: Juliana's Pizza
stops.note: Coal-fired, better than Grimaldi's next door. Lunch before heading north

stops.name: Pete's Candy Store
stops.note: Free live music nightly in the back room. Very local Williamsburg dive. Check set times

stops.name: Beacon's Closet
stops.note: Vintage clothing, big Greenpoint location. Walk north from Williamsburg

stops.name: Westlight Rooftop
stops.note: 22nd floor of William Vale hotel. Stunning 360 views. On the way back south

stops.name: Almondine Bakery
stops.note: French bakery in DUMBO, steps from the hotel. Croissants and coffee

stops.name: Brooklyn Bridge Park
stops.note: Final morning walk along the waterfront. Manhattan skyline goodbye

stops.name: Kalustyan's
stops.note: 20 min subway from DUMBO. Worth the trip for spice gifts & legendary sandwiches — skip if short on time

stops.name: Leave for JFK
stops.note: Uber from hotel ~45-60 min to JFK T8. Aim to arrive by 18:40 for 21:40 flight

items.name: Greene St, SoHo
items.note: Best cast iron facades in the city. Cobblestones, fire escapes, great light. 2 min from hotel

items.name: Bleecker to Bank St
items.note: West Village brownstones, tree-lined, classic NYC
items.address: Bleecker St & Bank St, New York

items.name: Perry St to Grove St
items.note: Film-location pretty. Quiet, leafy, the NYC dream. Friends building on Bedford St

items.name: Elizabeth St, Nolita
items.note: Boutiques, cafés, and flower shops. Quieter than SoHo, more local. 5 min from hotel
items.address: Elizabeth St & Prince St, New York

items.name: Washington St, DUMBO
items.note: THE iconic Brooklyn Bridge between buildings shot. Cobblestones, converted warehouses, best at golden hour

items.name: Bedford Ave, Williamsburg
items.note: Brooklyn's main drag. Vintage shops, street art, coffee. Walk north from Metropolitan to N 10th

items.name: Cranberry St, Brooklyn Heights
items.note: Gorgeous brownstones, quiet, near the Promenade. Perfect morning walk from 1 Hotel

items.name: Avenue B & Tompkins Square
items.note: East Village community gardens, punk rock history, local coffee shops. Raw NYC energy
items.address: Avenue B & E 7th St, New York

items.name: Stone St, Financial District
items.note: Cobblestoned pedestrian street with outdoor dining. Old Dutch NYC

items.name: Freeman Alley, LES
items.note: Hidden alley off Rivington. Graffiti, speakeasy entrance, very moody. Near hotel

items.name: Staple St, Tribeca
items.note: Tiny one-block alley with a skybridge. Most photographed hidden street in NYC. 10 min walk from hotel

items.name: Attaboy
items.note: No menu — tell them what you like. LES speakeasy legend

items.name: Apotheke
items.note: Apothecary-themed cocktails in old Chinatown opium den

items.name: Please Don't Tell (PDT)
items.note: Enter through phone booth in Crif Dogs. Reservations essential

items.name: The Campbell
items.note: Grand Central's hidden bar. Painted ceilings, old-money glamour

items.name: Dear Irving on Hudson
items.note: 39th floor of Aliz Hotel. Themed rooms, jaw-dropping views

items.name: Temple Bar
items.note: Speakeasy vibes, no sign outside. Dress smart. Strong martinis

items.name: Dante
items.note: World's Best Bar multiple years running. Negronis are the move. MacDougal St

items.name: Dead Rabbit
items.note: Multi-floor cocktail bar in FiDi. Irish-inspired, award-winning. Taproom downstairs

items.name: Bemelmans Bar
items.note: Live piano, Madeline murals on the walls. Old-money Upper East Side glamour

items.name: Rudy's Bar & Grill
items.note: Legendary Hell's Kitchen dive. Free hot dogs, cheap pitchers. Pure NYC

items.name: Jimmy
items.note: 18th-floor rooftop at ModernHaus SoHo. Pool deck, fireplace, fashion crowd. Indoor lounge open year-round

items.name: Nubeluz
items.note: 50th-floor cocktail bar at Ritz-Carlton NoMad. Floor-to-ceiling windows, dazzling skyline. Great mid-March pick

items.name: 230 Fifth
items.note: Massive rooftop with heated igloos in winter and Empire State Building views. Indoor penthouse lounge too

items.name: Spyglass
items.note: Intimate Midtown rooftop at Archer Hotel. Empire State Building as backdrop. Retractable enclosure in winter

items.name: The Skylark
items.note: Sleek 30th-floor lounge in Garment District. Dramatic Midtown views. Indoor lounge year-round

items.name: Refinery Rooftop
items.note: Industrial-chic with retractable glass roof. Empire State views. Enclosed and heated in winter

items.name: Ophelia
items.note: Art Deco cocktail lounge on 26th floor of Beekman Tower. East River and Chrysler Building views. Fully indoor

items.name: The Roof at Public Hotel
items.note: Ian Schrager's trendy rooftop on LES. Downtown crowd. Seasonal — may be closed mid-March, check ahead

items.name: Bar 54
items.note: Highest hotel rooftop bar in NYC. 54th floor at Hyatt Times Square. 360° Midtown views. Indoor lounge year-round

items.name: Strand Bookstore
items.note: 18 miles of books. Rare books room upstairs, tote bags as souvenirs

items.name: Grand Central Whispering Gallery
items.note: Stand in opposite corners of the archway and whisper. It works

items.name: The Vessel (Hudson Yards)
items.note: Honeycomb staircase sculpture. Free timed tickets

items.name: Smorgasburg Williamsburg
items.note: Outdoor food market, Saturdays only (Mar 14 on this trip). 100+ vendors

items.name: Brooklyn Flea
items.note: Vintage, antiques, handmade. Weekends, check location before going

items.name: Village Vanguard
items.note: The most legendary jazz club in the world. Operating since 1935. Book ahead

items.name: Mezzrow
items.note: Intimate piano jazz bar below street level. Same owners as Smalls, more chill

items.name: Dizzy's Club
items.note: Jazz at Lincoln Center. Floor-to-ceiling Central Park views. Worth the splurge

items.name: Nublu
items.note: Eclectic live music — jazz, funk, Afrobeat. East Village, very local crowd

items.name: Barbès
items.note: Tiny Park Slope bar, world music and jazz. $10 suggested donation. Beloved by locals

items.name: Russ & Daughters
items.note: Bagels & lox since 1914. The classic NYC breakfast. Go early to avoid the line

items.name: Prince Street Pizza
items.note: The pepperoni square is iconic. Crispy cups of pepperoni, worth the queue

items.name: Superiority Burger
items.note: Best veggie burger in NYC, maybe anywhere. Tiny East Village spot, big flavour

items.name: Lucali
items.note: BYOB pizza in Carroll Gardens. Jay-Z's favourite. Cash only, expect a wait

items.name: Scarr's Pizza
items.note: LES slice joint. House-milled flour, old-school vibes. Locals swear by it

items.name: Thai Diner
items.note: Thai-American diner mashup in Nolita. Khao soi french dip is unreal

items.name: Court Street Grocers
items.note: Best deli sandwiches in Brooklyn. Carroll Gardens & DUMBO locations

items.name: Banh Mi Saigon
items.note: Best banh mi in Chinatown. Crispy baguette, $6. Quick and perfect

items.name: L'Industrie Pizzeria
items.note: Williamsburg's best slice. Burrata slice is legendary. Always a line, always worth it

items.name: Los Tacos No.1
items.note: Best tacos in Manhattan. Adobada and nopal are the move. Also in Chelsea Market

items.name: Hanon
items.note: Tiny Japanese cream puff shop in West Village. Pistachio is incredible. Instagram queue but worth it

items.name: Vanessa's Dumpling House
items.note: 4 dumplings for $3. Chinatown institution, no frills. Fried pork dumplings are the one

items.name: Punjabi Grocery & Deli
items.note: 24-hour bodega-style Indian food on Houston. $7 combo plate, taxi drivers' favourite

items.name: Di Fara Pizza
items.note: Dom DeMarco's legendary Midwood slice. Worth the trek to Brooklyn. Cash only

items.name: Taqueria Al Pastor
items.note: Tiny East Harlem spot. Legit al pastor with pineapple. $3 tacos, pure Mexico City vibes

items.name: Ops
items.note: Bushwick natural wine bar with wood-fired pizza. Local date-night spot, great vibe

items.name: Win Son
items.note: Taiwanese-American brunch & dinner in Williamsburg. Fly's head popcorn chicken is unreal

items.name: Gigi's
items.note: Cobble Hill neighbourhood Italian. House focaccia, natural wines. Very local crowd

items.name: La Cabra
items.note: Scandinavian specialty coffee, great pastries. Lafayette St, SoHo

items.name: Felix Roasting Co
items.note: Beautiful interiors, great for photos. Order the oat latte. Flatiron

items.name: Birch Coffee
items.note: Local NYC chain, solid flat white. Multiple locations

items.name: Round K
items.note: Hong Kong–style coffee and egg waffles. Right by Chinatown

items.name: Maman
items.note: French-inspired café, locals love it. Great pastries. Multiple locations

items.name: Butler Coffee
items.note: Excellent cold brew, small but cosy. DUMBO waterfront

items.name: Almondine Bakery
items.note: French bakery in DUMBO. Croissants and coffee, steps from Brooklyn Bridge

items.name: Devoción
items.note: Colombian coffee roasted in-house. Stunning Williamsburg greenhouse space

items.name: Abraço
items.note: Tiny East Village espresso bar. Olive oil cake is legendary. Cash only

items.name: Sey Coffee
items.note: Bushwick roaster, minimalist Scandi vibes. One of NYC's best pourover spots

items.name: Comedy Cellar
items.note: The one. Chappelle, Schumer, CK all drop in to test material. Book online, sells out fast

items.name: Comedy Cellar at the Village Underground
items.note: Sister venue, same calibre lineups. Slightly bigger room, easier to get tickets

items.name: The Stand
items.note: Gramercy spot, great food and full bar. More upscale room, strong lineups

items.name: Gotham Comedy Club
items.note: Chelsea. Polished room, bigger names. Good date-night comedy spot

items.name: New York Comedy Club
items.note: East Village. Cheaper tickets, raw energy. Late shows get wild

items.name: Eastville Comedy Club
items.note: Intimate basement in the East Village. $5–10 shows, surprisingly great acts

items.name: ZARA SoHo
items.note: Massive flagship, three floors. Great basics and seasonal pieces

items.name: & Other Stories
items.note: Scandi-chic, affordable elevated basics. SoHo location is beautifully laid out

items.name: COS
items.note: Minimalist Scandi design, great tailoring at mid-range prices

items.name: Aritzia
items.note: Canadian brand, great coats and everyday pieces. Super Puff jacket is iconic

items.name: APC
items.note: French minimalism. Perfect jeans, clean lines. Worth a look even just to browse

items.name: Rag & Bone
items.note: NYC-born brand. Great leather jackets, denim, and knitwear

items.name: The Row
items.note: Olsen twins' ultra-luxe label. Appointment-level quiet luxury. Worth window shopping

items.name: Beacon's Closet
items.note: Huge Greenpoint vintage warehouse. Best selection in the city, well-organised

items.name: L Train Vintage
items.note: Multiple locations. Williamsburg & Bushwick spots are best. Priced by colour tag

items.name: Procell
items.note: Curated high-end vintage on LES. Designer archive pieces, 80s/90s gems

items.name: What Goes Around Comes Around
items.note: SoHo institution. Vintage Chanel, Levi's, band tees. Pricey but pristine

items.name: Metropolis Vintage
items.note: East Village. Rotating racks of 70s–90s pieces. Great leather jackets and denim

items.name: 10 Ft Single by Stella Dallas
items.note: Williamsburg staple. Affordable vintage, fun prints, great outerwear

items.name: McNally Jackson Books
items.note: Independent bookshop with gorgeous stationery section. SoHo

items.name: Strand Bookstore
items.note: 18 miles of books. Rare books room upstairs, tote bags as souvenirs

items.name: MoMA Design Store
items.note: Beautifully curated design objects, gifts, art prints. Across from the museum

items.name: Evolution Store
items.note: Natural history curiosities — fossils, insects in resin, anatomical models. SoHo weirdness

items.name: Kalustyan's
items.note: Legendary spice emporium. 5,000+ spices, condiments, and pantry goods. Gift heaven

items.name: Forbidden Planet
items.note: Iconic comic book and sci-fi megastore. Union Square. Great for nerdy souvenirs

items.name: ABC Carpet & Home
items.note: Six floors of eclectic home goods, jewellery, and curiosities. Union Square landmark

items.name: Artists & Fleas
items.note: Indie market in Williamsburg and Chelsea. Handmade jewellery, art, vintage. Weekends best

items.name: Bloomingdale's
items.note: Iconic UES flagship. Massive beauty floor, great shoe department. Get the Little Brown Bag

items.name: Saks Fifth Avenue
items.note: Classic luxury department store. Worth a walk through even just for the building

items.name: Nordstrom NYC
items.note: Newest flagship, 7 floors near Columbus Circle. Great menswear and shoe floors

items.name: Bergdorf Goodman
items.note: The most glamorous store in NYC. Window displays are legendary. 5th Ave at 58th

items.name: Century 21
items.note: Designer discount department store. FiDi flagship reopened. Treasure hunt energy

items.name: Macy's Herald Square
items.note: World's largest store. Wooden escalators, holiday windows. Touristy but iconic
